title: AgentOps Re-Steer Policy v1
description: Policy controlling when and how ao goals re-steer proposes or applies GOALS.md directive mutations. Stored at docs/re-steer-policy.json (or a path passed via --policy). See docs/adr/ADR-0006-re-steer-policy-and-mutation-safety.md for the full contract.

$schema.description: Optional self-reference to the schema URI for editor tooling.

minimum_evidence_count.description: Minimum number of completed ao-goals-measure iterations (ledger records) a directive must have before it is eligible for any mutation proposal. Prevents premature decisions on sparse data. An iteration is one completed ao goals measure run that records a scenario_verdict for the directive.

failure_streak_length.description: Number of consecutive 'fail' scenario_verdict values required before a directive is eligible for a mutation proposal. MUST be >= 2 — a single fresh failure must never trigger an applied mutation (ADR-0006 I-1). The schema minimum: 2 enforces this invariant machine-checkably.

cooldown_iterations.description: Number of iterations a directive must cool down after a mutation proposal or application before it is eligible again. Iteration count is ledger-based (one completed ao goals measure run = one iteration), not time-based.

allowed_mutation_types.description: Which mutation types the policy permits. 'priority_bump' moves the directive up in priority (bounded by max_priority_bump). 'setpoint_tighten' tightens the **Setpoint:** target; 'setpoint_loosen' loosens it. 'steer_flip' inverts **Steer:** direction and requires BOTH this entry AND allow_steer_flip:true (ADR-0006 I-3). 'steer_flip' is intentionally absent from the default.

max_priority_bump.description: Maximum number of positions a directive may be moved up in priority during a single re-steer event. Prevents a single failing directive from monopolizing the top of GOALS.md.

auto_apply.description: When false (default), mutations are recommendations only: printed to stdout and written to a proposal file, but GOALS.md is never modified. When true, mutations are applied to GOALS.md via the non-lossy patcher after the operator confirms interactively (or unconditionally in --non-interactive mode). ADR-0006 I-2: default behavior is recommendation-only.

allow_steer_flip.description: Hard safety gate for Steer-direction flips (ADR-0006 I-3). When false (default), the steer_flip mutation type is never performed regardless of allowed_mutation_types. When true, steer_flip must ALSO appear in allowed_mutation_types for any flip to occur (dual opt-in). A failing directive does not prove the optimization direction is inverted; Steer flips require explicit human policy opt-in.
